steps.title: Identify the action in the examples
steps.content: The examples show artists blending: Samantha Crain “incorporated... into” folk-pop, and Black Belt Eagle Scout “combined... with” rock. These verbs reveal the central action being described: merging Indigenous music with other musical styles.

steps.title: Match the blending action to the choices
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean "blend or combine" — the examples show artists merging Indigenous music with other styles. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — exaggerate: "artists exaggerate Indigenous music with other styles" — exaggerate means to overstate, not to mix genres together. ✗
  • Choice B — analyze: "artists analyze Indigenous music with other styles" — analyze means to study or examine, not to merge traditions. ✗
  • Choice C — fuse: "artists fuse Indigenous music with other styles" — fuse means to blend distinct things into one, exactly what Crain and Black Belt Eagle Scout do. ✓
  • Choice D — acquire: "artists acquire Indigenous music with other styles" — acquire means to obtain, which does not describe blending two musical traditions. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
